title: Corunastylis tecta
intro: Corunastylis tecta, also known as the hidden helmet orchid, is a rare and endangered species of orchid native to Australia. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine to alleviate respiratory issues and skin conditions, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Corunastylis tecta include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas and infusions, as well as extracts used in topical creams and ointments.
taxonomy: Corunastylis tecta is classified as a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae and the genus Corunastylis. Corunastylis tecta is native to Australia and is a type of epiphytic orchid. This species is part of the subtribe Thelychrtidinae within the Orchidaceae family. Corunastylis tecta is also known as the hidden sun orchid due to its unique habit of growing in shaded areas.
